ba_name: Andalusia Stryker Texas Smoked Single Malt Whiskey
reasoning: Texas grain-to-glass single malt where the malted barley is smoked with oak, mesquite, and apple woods (Texas BBQ tradition, NOT peat). Aged in charred American oak. 50% ABV. Nose: caramel + BBQ smoke. Heavy smoke from the BBQ-wood treatment maps to smoke=3; charred new oak adds vanilla.

100% malted barley, aged in bourbon casks then finished in sherry + peated casks. 45% ABV. The peated finish adds smoke but only partially — full-strength peated maturation would be higher. Sherry adds fruit/sweet.

ba_name: Griffo x Lagunitas Still Waldos Single Malt Whiskey
reasoning: Hop-influenced single malt — distilled from Lagunitas Waldos beer mash with hops. 5 yrs American oak. 42% ABV. Cherry-picked into ASM via extra_bottle_ids since BA path puts it under Hop-Influenced Whiskey (363/370/470/) alongside the unrelated Seven Stills experimentals. The hop character adds floral/citrus/herbal bitterness that the axes don't fully capture — like Crab Trapper, this is a quirky bottle that might rank in-pattern without conveying its actual weirdness.

ba_name: Wolves 5 Year American Single Malt Whiskey
reasoning: Aged 5 yrs in California wine barrels under Arizona heat (intense extraction) + Cognac cask finish. 48% ABV. Explicit tasting: honey, graham cracker, vanilla custard, nutmeg, clove, sweet oats, fig, ripe red fruit. No peat or smoke. The wine + cognac drive sweet/vanilla/fruit all to max.
